i think there's a nontrivial chance that none of the current frontier advanced meditation researchers will make any of the Future Big Breakthroughs towards mass-scale profound wellbeing (or, even if we do, we'll probably need to recruit lots of help along the way) -- we are so early, and everything in the field is so protoscientific right now. so, in some sense, a lot of research work in this field is just laying some good groundwork for the *future's* meditation researchers

but the problem is that those future researchers won't exist unless we do a really good job of sharing this stuff to them

fundamental research is incredibly important to do right now, but it's also really important to do a really skillful job of talking about this stuff!!!

especially in a world where thinking is getting increasingly commoditized (with ai models getting more capable of novel science), perhaps the most important leverage point for this field is making it legible/legitimate to curious people in the first place

that's what i'm trying to do with my blog post series, albeit it's just a first attempt.

we have a real opportunity here to accelerate the field by getting really good at making it legible and legitimate to curious nerds.

i'd make a different argument if the meditation research field was bigger, or if this field already had more legitimate connotations, or if nerds already thought that this field is cool -- but none of these things are true at a big scale yet.

there is so much low-hanging fruit here, and this won't happen unless we actively try to get better at it!

yeah it turns out this is like the meditation equivalent of going to a tennis coach every day but not fundamentally improving bc you’re just hitting the ball and not really debugging the fundamental micromovements that matter

2.5 trying to remove bad emotions via trying to fight/delete/repress/shoo them

it turns out this is just super ineffective, and the way to dissolve these things is more about letting them pass without constipating it (again, it’s about less resistance) and bringing them to the light and putting awareness on it (which sometimes can look like “love”)

2. trying to remove bad emotions

it turns out suffering is produced by our resistance to things, not the actual bad emotion, and so the goal isnt to remove bad emotions (youre still human) but to lower resistance/clinging/attachment

and not in a philosophical way but like literally physiologically there are particular 10-40hz graspy clingy mental motions which turn out to be the cause of ~all suffering, which we can learn to investigate and undo

(and this is what “desire is the root of all suffering” really means, but it’s widely misinterpreted bc of bad translation — it’s not a philosophical argument, it’s super verifiable via meditation bc of things like sensory clarity)
